user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: Clean cinematic science visualization, realistic aircraft wing, subtle airflow particles, high-contrast educational overlays
style_bible.camera_rules: Camera remains outside aircraft, smooth deliberate moves, clear view of airflow over wing
style_bible.lighting_rules: Natural daylight, soft sun from above and slightly behind camera, no harsh shadows
style_bible.continuity_rules: Same airplane wing, altitude, sky, and camera screen direction maintained across scenes

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Introduce airflow around an airplane wing and show speed difference above and below. GLOBAL CONTINUITY: Primary subject is a single airplane wing seen from outside in level flight, high altitude blue sky with thin clouds. Camera tracks left-to-right parallel to wing. Explanatory state: introducing air splitting and faster flow above.
SHOT 1 (0–3s): framing: wide profile of wing from slightly front-left; lens: 35mm; camera movement: slow dolly alongside; subject & action: by 1s the wing enters frame, by 2s airflow particles approach, by 3s air visibly splits over and under the wing; on-screen text overlay: "Wing meets air"; environment details: blue sky, distant clouds, faint contrails; lighting: soft daylight from upper right; cinematic look: crisp realism, moderate depth of field.
SHOT 2 (3–7s): framing: medium close-up of wing cross-section area; lens: 50mm; camera movement: static hold; subject & action: airflow particles trace paths, top stream stretches and accelerates while bottom remains steadier; on-screen text overlay: "Air splits"; environment details: sky gradient, subtle motion blur in particles; lighting: even soft light; cinematic look: clean scientific clarity.
SHOT 3 (7–10s): framing: close-up along top surface of wing; lens: 85mm; camera movement: slow pan following airflow; subject & action: faster-moving particles streak along curved top surface, emphasizing speed; on-screen text overlay: "Faster flow above"; environment details: wing rivets, sky reflections; lighting: gentle highlight along wing curve; cinematic look: shallow depth of field, smooth filmic motion.
scenes.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text
scenes.camera: Exterior tracking shot parallel to wing, left-to-right screen direction maintained
scenes.audio_cue: Off-screen narration explaining air splitting and faster flow over the wing

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Explain pressure differences and how they create lift, continuing from established airflow. GLOBAL CONTINUITY: Same wing, altitude, sky, and left-to-right camera direction as Scene 1. Explanatory state: faster flow above already shown; now linking speed to pressure and lift.
SHOT 1 (0–3s): framing: medium shot of wing top and bottom visible; lens: 35mm; camera movement: static hold; subject & action: airflow particles above move fast and thin out, below move slower and denser; on-screen text overlay: "Lower pressure"; environment details: blue sky, thin clouds, wing underside shadow; lighting: soft daylight from above; cinematic look: balanced exposure, educational clarity.
SHOT 2 (3–7s): framing: medium close-up under the wing; lens: 50mm; camera movement: slow tilt upward; subject & action: denser particles press upward under wing, visual arrows subtly indicating force; on-screen text overlay: "Higher pressure"; environment details: sky gradient, wing underside details; lighting: gentle reflected light; cinematic look: smooth motion, moderate depth of field.
SHOT 3 (7–10s): framing: wide profile of entire wing; lens: 35mm; camera movement: crane slightly upward then hold; subject & action: wing subtly rises relative to airflow streams, particles curve downward behind wing; on-screen text overlay: "Lift upward"; environment details: open sky, distant clouds; lighting: soft sun rim light on wing edge; cinematic look: cinematic realism with clear final hold frame.
scenes.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text
scenes.camera: Exterior wide shot, slight upward crane ending in static hold, same direction and distance as prior scene
scenes.audio_cue: Off-screen narration explaining pressure differences creating lift

full_script: 0–2s Narration: "An airplane wing shapes the air as it moves." On-screen text: "Wing meets air". 2–5s Narration: "Air splits above and below the wing." On-screen text: "Air splits". 5–10s Narration: "The curved top makes air move faster." On-screen text: "Faster flow above". 10–13s Narration: "Faster air means lower pressure." On-screen text: "Lower pressure". 13–17s Narration: "Slower air below stays higher pressure." On-screen text: "Higher pressure". 17–20s Narration: "Pressure difference lifts the wing." On-screen text: "Lift upward".
